rationale: Exploit our current military advantage and enemy war fatigue with a precision strike to degrade their command nodes and shatter morale.
Conduct a high-visibility naval show of force near contested waters to deter allied naval escalation and reinforce regional dominance.
Increase border and rear-area patrols to prevent sabotage and maintain control over occupied territory amid rising instability.
Launch an offensive cyber operation to disrupt Tethyan military logistics and C2 systems, exploiting the high crisis level and their dependence on networked defenses.
Conduct covert sabotage on key supply depots in recently captured zones to delay Tethyan counteroffensive preparations.
Seed false narratives about leadership disunity and imminent surrender to amplify war fatigue and erode domestic resistance.
Accelerate stockpiling of critical commodities to insulate our economy from ongoing sanctions and price volatility.
Issue emergency war bonds to finance military operations and offset fiscal strain caused by sustained conflict and sanctions.
Quietly engage neutral powers to secure alternative trade routes and weaken the cohesion of the sanctioning coalition.
With Novaris holding a moderate military advantage and controlling nearly a quarter of our territory, reinforcing vulnerable sectors is essential to prevent further breakthroughs.
A show of force in regional waters following the joint naval exercises deters amphibious escalation and reaffirms control over maritime approaches.
Precision strikes against overstretched supply depots disrupt logistics, exploiting confirmed strains in enemy supply lines.
At crisis level 8 and with over 20% territory lost, offensive sabotage against key rail junctions and forward supply caches degrades Novaris’ operational tempo.
In response to their cyber accusations and to offset conventional weakness, disruptive cyber operations against military logistics systems create strategic friction.
Leverage war fatigue in Novaris by amplifying narratives of rising casualties and supply shortages to undermine domestic support.
With international support at 65%, now is the time to institutionalize alliances through formal coordination mechanisms to sustain pressure on Novaris.
Establishing monitored aid routes highlights our moral leadership and draws global attention to occupation conditions, bolstering diplomatic leverage.
Quietly explore conditional disengagement terms through neutral intermediaries to manage escalation risks from recent air and naval incidents.
With sanctions severity above 50%, we must secure critical energy and food reserves to insulate the economy from further external shocks and war-induced shortages.
Mobilize domestic capital to fund defense spending and offset declining public patience, turning economic anxiety into patriotic investment.
Coordinate with allies to restrict dual-use technology exports, amplifying the economic strain on Novaris’ overstretched military machine.
